titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.28

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=684 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:52:47.822432+00 | translation moa=685 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:52:49.865711+00

div.p:
Sciant vniuersi Sancte Matris Ecclesie filii tam presentes quam futuri . Quod cum lis mota fuisset . et diutius ventilata in capitulo Orientali Laudonie inter
dominum . I . Priorem et conuentum Sancti Andree ex una parte . et Magistrum et Moniales de Hadigtona ex altera super diuersis decimis Maioribus et
Minoribus hinc Inde petitis : Scilicet quod cum decime carrucate terre in territorio Helfstanford que quondam spectabat ad territorium
de drem . Et decime duarum bouatarum terre contentarum in ueteri gardino de Stefnistona . Et decime illius terre que iacet inter vetus gardinum .
et villam de hadigtona . et decime dimidie illius terre iuxta molendinum de Stefinistona . et decime Croftorum quorundam burgensium de
hadigtona . quas tunc temporis Magister et Moniales tenebant ex parte prioris et Conuentus de Sancto Andrea prefatis Monialibus peterentur 
et ex parte Magistri et Monialium de Hadigtona Minute decime de biris . et Cotariorum de biris et de Bernis a predictis Canonicis exigerentur
in iure . ac lite Super prefatis petitionibus hinc Inde legitime contestata . Et testibus ex vtraque parte productis et examinatis et attesta
tionibus publicatis et inspectis . atque allegationibus vtriusque partis auditis et intellectis . et inspectis instrumentis eorundem : Cum nichil resta
bat nisi sentencia ferenda . Tandem post multorum labores et vexationes indebitas  partes per procuratores legittime constitutos ad com
ponendum et transigendum Die Sabbati post ad vincula Sancti Petri in Ecclesia de Lauheder . Anno gracie Millesimo ducentesimo quadragesimo quinto Coram Magistro Andrea
de Aberden Officiali domini Episcopi Sancti Andree comparuerunt presentibus viris discretis . Domino . I . abbate de Driburc . et Magistris Willelmo de gren
lau . et Adam de Malkaruistona . et Henrico de holtal et aliis probis hominibus Clericis et laicis Religionis et Secularibus partes suas ad pa
cis reformationem interponentibus lis inter partes mota ac diucius agitata in hac forma amicabiliter conquieuit . videlicet quod Petrus
Canonicus procurator Canonicorum Sancti Andree remisit et quietas clamauit nomine dictorum prioris et Conuentur Sancti Andree predictis Monialibus de
hadigtona . omnes decimas superius nominatas ita quod dicte Moniales prefatas decimas decetero percipiant in perpetuum et pacifice
possideant . et in vsus proprios sine aliqua contradictione conuertant . Pro qua remissione et quieta Clamatione prefati Magister et Monia
les dederunt sepedictis Canonicis de Sancto Andree redditum . viginti . solidorum . in decimis de faueside in parrochia de karel . Et quia
omnes prefate decime garbarum de faueside estimate fuerunt per visum proborum et fidelium hominum ex vtraque parte electorum
et iuratorum quod plus valebant in septemdecim Solidos quam Redditus viginti solidorum prefatis Canonicis assignatus . Et de communi con
sensu et assensu vtriusque partis prouisum fuit quod prenominati Canonici persoluant prenominatis Monialibus Septemdecim solidos
annuatim ad Pascha . pro residuo dictarum Decimarum . Et ita prefati Canonici omnes Decimas garbarum de faueside in perpetuum
possidebunt . et in vusus proprios Licite conuertent . Et vt ista pacis Reformatio . et gratuita compositio Robur perpetue firmitatis
optineat . et ne per maliciam et Calliditates Malignancium Lis ita amicabiliter Sopita iterum valeat suscitari  vtraque pars
tam Canonicorum quam Monialium Subiecit se iurisdicioni Episcopi Sancti Andree qui pro tempore fuerit sic Quod possit vna sola
ammonitione premissa partem retinentem vel nolentem istam compositionem obseruare . vt fideliter ipsam perpetuo obseruet
per Censuram ecclesiasticam Compellere . Et insuper ad Maiorem securitatem . et vt omnis ambiguitas tollatur parti Scripture
in Modum Cyrographi confecte . et remanenti penes Canonicos de Sancto Andrea Sigillum Monialium de Hadigtona vna Cum
sigillo domini Dauid . dei gratia Episcopi Sancti Andree . Et Magistri Andree Officialis dicti Epicopi sunt apposita . Et parti remanenti penes monia
les Sigillum Capituli Sancti Andree . et dicti Episcopi . et Officialis sunt appensa . Teste toto Capitulo Laudonie . Coram quo lec
ta fuit hec Compositio . et a partibus approbata

p.cl: Let all sons of Holy Mother Church know, those present as well as those to come after, that

when a dispute at law was initiated and deliberated for a long time in the chapter-house of East Lothian between lord John the prior and the community of St Andrews on the one side, and the master and nuns of Haddington on the other, over various teinds, great and small, claimed on either side, that is to say, when the teinds of a ploughgate of land in the territory of Athelstaneford which once belonged to the territory of Drem, and the teinds of two oxgangs of land contained in the old garden of Stevenson, and the teinds of that land which lies between the old garden and the toun of Haddington, and the teinds of half of that land next to the mill of Stevenson, and the teinds of the crofts belonging to certain burgesses of Haddington, which the master and nuns were then holding, were claimed on the part of the prior and community of St Andrews from the aforementioned nuns; and on the part of the master and nuns of Haddington, the small teinds of Byres, and the cottaries of Byres and of Barney were demanded in law; and the dispute in law over the aforementioned claims on both sides having been lawfully asserted, and the witnesses on either side having been produced and examined, and evidence having been published and inspected, and with allegations on either side having been heard and understood, and having inspected the instruments of the same, when nothing remained except for the judgment to be rendered, at length after many labours and undue troubles, the parties through their agents lawfully constituted to settle and complete [the case] on Saturday after St Peter’s Chains in the church of Lauder in the year of grace 1245 before of Master Andrew of Aberdeen, official of the lord bishop of St Andrews, they compeared with the men of discretion present, the lord John, abbot of Dryburgh, Master William of Greenlaw, Adam of Maxton, Henry of Holtal, and other worthy men, clerk and lay, of religion and secular, representing their own parties for the restoration of peace, the suit initiated between the parties, and for a long time pursued, was in this form amicably settled, namely, that Peter the canon, agent of the canons of St Andrews, remitted and quitclaimed in the name of the said prior and canons of St Andrews to the aforesaid nuns of Haddington all the teinds named above so that the said nuns may in future receive and possess undisturbed the aforementioned teinds for ever, and turn them to their own use without any disagreement. In return for this remission and quitclaim, the aforesaid master and nuns have given to the oft-mentioned canons of St Andrews a rent of twenty shillings in the teinds of Falside in the parish of Crail. And since all the aforementioned garbal teinds of Falside have been reckoned by the inspection of worthy and faithful men from both sides, of the electors and the jurors, that they were worth seventeen shillings more than the rent of twenty shillings assigned to the aforementioned canons, by joint approval and agreement of either side it was laid down that the aforenamed canons would pay to the aforenamed nuns seventeen shillings annually at Easter for the remainder of the said teinds. And so, the aforementioned canons shall forever possess, and lawfully turn over to their own purposes, all the garbal teinds. And in order that this restoration of peace and gracious agreement may gain the validity of everlasting endurance, and lest by the malice and craftiness of wicked men the legal suit thus amicably settled could again be revived, either side, the canons as much as the nuns, have submitted themselves to the jurisdiction of the one who is at the time bishop of St Andrews, so that it be possible to compel by one single warning through ecclesiastical censure the party witholding the foregoing things or unwilling to abide by this agreement to observe it faithfully for ever through ecclesiastical censure.

And in addition, for greater security, and so that all uncertainty may be removed, to the side of the document – produced in the manner of a cyrograph – remaining in the possession of the canons of St Andrews, the seal of the nuns of Haddington together with the seal of the lord David, by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews, and of Master Andrew, official of the said bishop, have been applied; and to the side remaining in the possession of the nuns the seal of the chapter of St Andrews, and of the said bishop, and of the official, have been attached.
As witness the whole chapter of Lothian, before whom this agreement was read out and approved by the parties.
